free_text: Electric and magnetic dipole strength in 66Zn
University of the West of Scotland, Paisley
University of Strathclyde, Glasgow
Samples with mass of 1.4993 g
in disk form with diameter of 20 mm.
At ELBE the target was combined
with 0.300 g of boron, enriched in 11B
Four high-purity Germanium detectors with 100%
efficiency were mounted around the target. All HPGe
detectors were surrounded by escape-suppression BGO
scintillation shields of 3 cm in thickness. Two HPGe
detectors were placed at 127deg relative to the
photon beam direction at 32 cm from the target. Two
HPGe detectors were positioned in a horizontal plane
at 90deg to the beam at 28 cm from the target.
Compiled by S.H.
Experiment was performed at the
photon scattering facility at the superconducting
electron accelerator ELBE (gammaELBE) in Dresden
Bremsstrahlung was produced by electron beams of
energy 7.5 and 13.4 MeV. At 7.5 MeV the radiator was Nb
foil 5 mu-m thick. At 13.4 MeV the radiator was Nb foil
12.5 mu-m thick. At 13.4 MeV A 10 cm thick aluminum
absorber was placed behind the radiator to reduce the
low-energy intensity of the bremsstrahlung.
Origin of uncertainties not described
Data taken from tab.1 of the reference PR/C
Experiment was performed at the
photon scattering facility at the superconducting
electron accelerator ELBE (gammaELBE) in Dresden
Bremsstrahlung was produced by electron beams of
energy 7.5 and 13.4 MeV. At 7.5 MeV the radiator was Nb
foil 5 mu-m thick. At 13.4 MeV the radiator was Nb foil
12.5 mu-m thick. At 13.4 MeV A 10 cm thick aluminum
absorber was placed behind the radiator to reduce the
low-energy intensity of the bremsstrahlung.
Origin of uncertainties not described
Data taken from tab.1 of the reference PR/C
Part of the measurements were
performed at HIgS facility at TUNL, Durham
Monoenergetic photon beams are produced at HIgS
by Compton back-scattering of a high-intensity free-
electron laser beam from an electron beam in the TUNL
storage ring.
Total uncertainty, which include
- statistical uncertainty
systematic uncertainty in detector efficiency
and simulated atomic background
- uncertainties in photon flux based on
uncertainties of the cross sections in 11B
Systematic uncertainties of level-density
models, not included in ERR-T
Data taken from tab.4 of the reference PR/C
